name: pboot
ide_drive: single -drive if=ide,format=raw,index=0,file=pboot.img (primary master); gen_disk.py places the 512-byte boot sector (pboot_mbr.bin) at LBA0 of BOTH pboot.img (qemu -drive) and pboot.disk.hex (ven_ide $readmemh) from one buffer (drift-asserted), the rest zero
what_this_is: M9 -- the FIRST real boot. The smallest thing that is genuinely 'booting': a reset-vector firmware (pboot_stub.bin, the -bios) that CHAIN-LOADS a real boot sector off the IDE disk and hands control to it. Unlike pide (a hand-written firmware that drives devices in place), pboot executes REAL boot code that was loaded from the disk at run time.
transitions: build a flat GDT @0x500 (null / code32 0x08 / data32 0x10), LGDT, set CR0.PE, far-jmp 0x08:pm32 -> 32-bit protected mode (flat segments, esp=0x90000)
set nIEN (0x3F6 bit1) FIRST -> polled, NO IRQ14 ever delivered -> the whole run is synchronous and per-record single-step differentiable (the same invariant the pide gate relies on)

READ SECTORS (0x20) disk LBA0, nsector=1, primary master LBA mode: poll BSY=0/DRQ=1 (status 0x58), drain 256 words via inw into RAM at 0x8000 (the boot sector); the disk LBA0 is the boot sector (last word 0xAA55 boot signature)

FAR JMP 0x08:0x8000 -> the firmware HANDS OFF to the disk-loaded boot sector (the BIOS->MBR handoff). GATE-ASSERTED non-vacuously: the trace MUST reach pc=0x00008000 (the boot sector executing from RAM), on BOTH the qemu golden and the ventium_soc RTL trace -- a silent early halt or a failed chain-load would never reach it
the boot sector EXECUTES FROM RAM (cs=0x08, flat 32-bit): writes a deterministic 64-bit signature (0x9000 <- 0xB007B007, 0x9004 <- 0x600DCAFE) then OUT 0x42 -> isa-debug-exit code 133
why_per_record_differential: Every step is a synchronous IN/OUT or polled-status read (nIEN set FIRST so no IRQ14 is ever raised), so qemu-system-i386 8.2.2's gdbstub single-step (gen_trace.py --system) is a valid per-record oracle. The gate is plain compare.py --mode func EQUIVALENT over all 1084 retired instructions, running on ventium_soc (the M8.x device set) with the matching -drive. Record 0 is the reset vector (pc=0xfff0/cs=0xf000); records ~1080+ are the boot sector executing at pc=0x8000.
self_modifying_fetch: The firmware WRITES the boot sector into RAM (0x8000) then far-jmps INTO it (fetch from just-written RAM). The per-record EQUIVALENT confirms ventium_soc handles this fetch-after-write with no I-cache/SMC hazard (both qemu and the RTL retire the loaded code identically). The pc=0x00008000 cross-trace assertion guards this.
zero_new_rtl: M9 added NO RTL: it runs on the existing ventium_soc + ven_ide PIO read path + the flat TB memory + the M8.x device set. It is pure boot CONTENT + a gate.
deferred_fuller_boot: A REAL firmware (SeaBIOS) POST + a real OS are a SEPARATE, much larger track, NOT single-step-differentiable today. Blocking gaps (research-confirmed): (ISA) CPUID is gated off under soc_en (a 1-line ungate), and far CALL (0x9A) / RETF (0xCA/0xCB) / LES/LDS (0xC4/0xC5) / LSS/LFS/LGS (0x0F B2/B4/B5) / RDTSC / WBINVD/INVD / SGDT/SIDT/SLDT/LLDT / CMPXCHG8B / ENTER / BOUND are unimplemented (loud HALT); (MEMORY/CHIPSET) no i440FX PAM shadow registers (0x58-0x5F) or a ROM-shadow-aware memory model for SeaBIOS's relocate-to-shadow-RAM step, no port-0x61 refresh-toggle, no 0xB8000/0xA0000 VGA framebuffer; (ORACLE) gen_trace single-steps with no main-loop pump, so IRQ-driven / async-block-AIO progress never advances, and a 100K-1M-instruction POST has no checkpoint -- needing a replay-log oracle (the repo's Win95 -d cpu mechanism) before any POST-scale or IRQ-driven boot can be gated. A 16-bit-real-mode boot sector at the canonical 0000:7C00 is a small differentiable follow-on (M9-rm; real-mode INT/IRET + far JMP are present); a bus-master DMA chain-load is another (M9b, reusing the M8.4f single-PRD READ DMA).
description: Bare-metal M9 SoC first-boot differential. The -bios firmware boots real->protected, sets nIEN, reads the boot sector from IDE LBA0 via synchronous PIO into RAM, and far-jmps into it; the disk-loaded boot sector then executes from RAM, writes a signature, and exits via isa-debug-exit. Proves the firmware->MBR boot handoff (a real boot sector loaded from the disk and executed) byte-identical to qemu-system-i386 over all 1084 retired instructions, with a non-vacuous pc=0x8000 handoff assertion on both traces. Zero new RTL -- runs on the existing ventium_soc device set.
